docs.title: Actor
docs.description: An Actor Stat Effect modifies Actor Stats.

docs.title: Effect
docs.description: An Effect defines a modification that can be applied to one or more target Stats.

docs.title: Effect Pack
docs.description: An Effect Pack Stat Effect modifies Effect Pack Stats.

docs.title: Effect Timer Modifier
docs.description: An Effect Timer Modifier inherits from Float Stat Effect.

docs.title: Injector
docs.description: Injectors are processed like any other Effect. After being processed, the Injector Model referenced by the Effect is added to the target Stat.

docs.title: Injector Remover
docs.description: It removes Injectors from the targeted Stat. Injectors can be removed selectively based on their tags and the Actor that originally added them.

docs.title: Linker
docs.description: Linkers are processed like any other Effect. After being processed, the referenced Linker Model is added to the target Stat.

docs.title: Modifier Remover
docs.description: It removes existing Modifiers from the targeted Stat. Modifiers can be removed selectively by configuring the provided filtering options.

docs.title: Float
docs.description: A Float Stat Effect modifies Float Stats.

docs.title: Numerable
docs.description: A Numerable Stat Effect is the base class for Effects that modify numerable Stats, such as Float Stat Effects and Int Stat Effects.

docs.title: Int
docs.description: An Int Stat Effect modifies Int Stats.

docs.title: String
docs.description: A String Stat Effect modifies String Stats.

docs.title: Vector
docs.description: A Vector Stat Effect is the base class for Effects that modify vector-based Stats, including Vector2, Vector3, Vector2Int, and Vector3Int Stats.

docs.title: Vector2
docs.description: A Vector2 Stat Effect modifies Vector2 Stats.

docs.title: Vector2Int
docs.description: A Vector2Int Stat Effect modifies Vector2Int Stats.

docs.title: Vector3
docs.description: A Vector3 Stat Effect modifies Vector3 Stats.

docs.title: Vector3Int
docs.description: A Vector3Int Stat Effect modifies Vector3Int Stats.

docs.title: Adapter
docs.description: Converts a Stat value between different types or modifies it into a format required by the target processor or injector.

docs.title: Actor Stat Extractor
docs.description: Returns a Stat from the ActorEntity contained within an ActorStat.

docs.title: Alter
docs.description: Returns the modified value of a Stat after applying a predefined operation.

docs.title: Alter Vector By Secondary
docs.description: Applies a predefined vector operation between the input vector Stat and a secondary vector Stat, then returns the resulting value.

docs.title: Chain
docs.description: Processes a Stat value through a sequence of StatValueAdapters, passing the output of each adapter as the input to the next.

docs.title: Distance By Source Vector
docs.description: Returns the distance between the input vector Stat and another vector retrieved from the specified source.

docs.title: Float To Int (Altered)
docs.description: Transforms a Float Stat into an Int value after applying a predefined modification.

docs.title: Stat Value Adapter
docs.description: Transforms a Stat value into a format suitable for further processing.

docs.title: Int To Float (Altered)
docs.description: Transforms an Int Stat into a Float value after applying a predefined modification.

docs.title: To Uniform Vector
docs.description: Converts the value of a Stat into a uniform vector, where all components are assigned the same value.

docs.title: To Vector Index
docs.description: Assigns the value of a Stat to a specified vector component while setting all other components to 0.

docs.title: Alter
docs.description: Modifies the input value using a predefined value.

docs.title: Chain
docs.description: Processes the input value through a sequence of ValueAdapters, passing the output of each adapter as the input to the next.

docs.title: Value Adapter
docs.description: Transforms an input value into a format suitable for further processing.

docs.title: Parent Actor
docs.description: Works with ActorEntity values.

docs.title: To Uniform Vector
docs.description: Converts the input value into a uniform vector, where all components are assigned the same value.

docs.title: To Vector Index
docs.description: Assigns the input value to a specified vector component while setting all other components to 0.

docs.title: Cross
docs.description: Returns the cross product of the input vector and a predefined vector.

docs.title: Direction
docs.description: Returns the normalized direction from the input vector to a predefined vector.

docs.title: Dot
docs.description: Returns the dot product of the input vector and a predefined vector.

docs.title: Normalize
docs.description: Returns the normalized version of the input vector.

docs.title: Project On Plane
docs.description: Projects the input vector onto a predefined plane and returns the projected vector.

docs.title: Reflect
docs.description: Returns the reflection of the input vector using a predefined normal vector.

docs.title: Subtract
docs.description: Subtracts a predefined vector from the input vector and returns the result.

docs.title: Injector
docs.description: Injectors transform a stat's value according to their configuration.

docs.title: Injector Definition Model
docs.description: Stores an InjectorModel and the order in which it will be injected.

docs.title: Absolute Value
docs.description: Sets the Stat value to a predefined injector value.

docs.title: Alter Modifiers
docs.description: Modifies the modifier values applied to a Stat.

docs.title: Combine
docs.description: Modifies the Stat value using the injected value according to the selected ContributionType.

docs.title: Injector Model
docs.description: Injects a value into a Stat.

docs.title: Limit
docs.description: Limits the Stat value according to the injected value and the configured limit definition.

docs.title: Stat Injector Definition Model
docs.description: Stores a StatInjectorModel and the order in which it will be injected.

docs.title: Absolute Value
docs.description: Sets the target Stat value to the value of the injector Stat.

docs.title: Add
docs.description: Adds the value of the injector Stat to the target Stat.

docs.title: Add Value By Condition
docs.description: Adds the value of the injector Stat to the target Stat only if the specified condition is met.

docs.title: Alter Modifiers
docs.description: Modifies the modifier values applied to the target Stat according to the configured definition.

docs.title: Eased Value
docs.description: Uses the injector Stat value as the easing duration, then multiplies the result by the EasedValue Stat.

docs.title: Stat Injector Model
docs.description: Injects the value of one Stat into another Stat.

docs.title: Insert Stat To SigSep Stat
docs.description: Used with SigSep Stats.

docs.title: Limit
docs.description: Limits the target Stat value according to the value of the injector Stat and the configured limit definition.

docs.title: Merge SigSep Stats
docs.description: Used with SigSep Stats.

docs.title: Multiply
docs.description: Multiplies the target Stat value by the value of the injector Stat.

docs.title: Multiply Value By Condition
docs.description: Multiplies the target Stat value by the value of the injector Stat only if the specified condition is met.

docs.title: Rate
docs.description: Applies the value of the injector Stat as a rate to the target Stat value.

docs.title: Rate Value By Condition
docs.description: Applies the value of the injector Stat as a rate to the target Stat value only if the specified condition is met.

docs.title: Premade Asset Pack
docs.description: Premade Asset Pack (PAP) is an editor asset designed to simplify the creation of frequently used assets that do not contain Stat relations.

docs.title: Premade Relation Pack
docs.description: Premade Relation Pack (PRP) is an editor asset designed to simplify the creation of frequently used Stat relation setups.

docs.title: Difference
docs.description: A Difference Condition subtracts one Stat value from another and evaluates the result using a comparison condition.

docs.title: Dispatcher Condition
docs.description: A Dispatcher Condition contains multiple conditions and evaluates the incoming Stats against all of them.

docs.title: Distance
docs.description: A Distance Condition calculates the distance between two Vector Stats and evaluates the result using a comparison condition.

docs.title: Dot
docs.description: A Dot Condition calculates the dot product of two Vector Stats and evaluates the result using a comparison condition.

docs.title: Multi Stat
docs.description: Multi Stat Conditions are used when a condition needs to evaluate multiple Stats instead of a single Stat.

docs.title: Sum
docs.description: A Sum Condition adds two Stat values together and evaluates the result using a comparison condition.

docs.title: Vector Index
docs.description: A Vector Index Condition compares the values of the selected component from two Vector Stats using a Numerable Comparer.

docs.title: Stat Condition
docs.description: A Stat Condition checks whether a Stat satisfies a specified condition.

docs.title: Multi Stat (Dynamic)
docs.description: Similar to MultiStatCondition, but instead of using predefined comparison values, all comparison values are retrieved dynamically from Stats.

docs.title: Actor Null Check
docs.description: An Actor Null Check verifies whether the Actor referenced by an Actor Stat exists.

docs.title: Numerable Comparer
docs.description: A Numerable Comparer evaluates whether a numerable value satisfies a comparison rule.

docs.title: String Comparer
docs.description: A String Comparer checks whether a string value is equal to another string value.

docs.title: Vector Index
docs.description: A Vector Index checks whether a specific component of a vector satisfies a comparison rule.

docs.title: Vector Magnitude
docs.description: A Vector Magnitude checks whether the magnitude of a vector satisfies a comparison rule.

docs.title: Single Stat (Dynamic)
docs.description: Unlike SingleStatCondition, this condition does not use a predefined comparison value. Instead, both the target value and the comparison value are retrieved dynamically from Stats.

docs.title: Stat Scaler
docs.description: Returns a multiplier calculated from one or more Stats.

docs.title: Distance
docs.description: Calculates the distance between two vector Stats, maps the result using a ValueMapper, and returns the resulting multiplier.

docs.title: Dot
docs.description: Calculates the dot product between two vector Stats, maps the result using a ValueMapper, and returns the resulting multiplier.

docs.title: Multi
docs.description: Calculates and returns a multiplier using multiple Stats.

docs.title: Single
docs.description: A StatScaler that calculates its multiplier using a single Stat.

docs.title: Map Value
docs.description: Maps a Stat value from one range to another and returns the resulting multiplier.

docs.title: Value Distributer
docs.description: Used by Reflector processors.

docs.title: Counter Effect
docs.description: The Counter Effect Processor triggers another Effect after the current Effect has been successfully applied.

docs.title: Postprocessors
docs.description: The Postprocessor stage contains all Processors that execute after an Effect has been successfully applied.

docs.title: Modifier Replacer
docs.description: The Modifier Replacer Processor replaces an existing Modifier when adding a new Modifier would exceed the configured replacement limit.

docs.title: Attribute Stat Eliminator
docs.description: The Attribute Stat Eliminator Processor determines which Attribute Stats should participate in processing for Attribute Stat Effects and Multi Attribute Stat Effects.

docs.title: Cast Restrictor
docs.description: The Cast Restrictor Processor determines whether the Source Actor is allowed to apply an Effect.

docs.title: Counter Effect
docs.description: The Counter Effect Processor triggers another Effect before the current Effect is applied.

docs.title: Director
docs.description: The Director Processor forwards the original, unprocessed Effect to another Actor before the current Effect continues through the processing pipeline.

docs.title: Effect Attribute Eliminator
docs.description: The Effect Attribute Eliminator Processor removes Effect Attributes before the Effect continues through the processing pipeline.

docs.title: Hit Rate
docs.description: The Hit Rate Processor determines whether an Effect successfully hits its target.

docs.title: Immune
docs.description: The Immune Processor determines whether an Effect is allowed to be applied.

docs.title: Preprocessor
docs.description: The Preprocessor stage runs before an Effect enters its main processing pipeline and before it is applied.

docs.title: Modifier Count Limit
docs.description: The Modifier Count Limit Processor limits how many Modifiers can be added to a Stat. If the configured limit has already been reached, the incoming Effect is blocked and the Modifier is not added.

docs.title: Add
docs.description: The Add Processor calculates the Effect value and adds the resulting value to the target Stat.

docs.title: Processors
docs.description: The Processor stage is the main stage of the processing pipeline where Effects are applied to their target Stats.

docs.title: Injector
docs.description: The Injector Processor processes Injector Effects and adds the configured Injector to the target Stat.

docs.title: Injector Remover
docs.description: The Injector Remover Processor processes Injector Remover Effects and removes Injectors from the target Stat according to the filtering rules defined by the Injector Remover Effect.

docs.title: Linker
docs.description: The Linker Processor processes Linker Effects and links another Stat to the target Stat by adding the configured Linker.

docs.title: Modifier Remover
docs.description: The Modifier Remover Processor processes Modifier Remover Effects and removes Modifiers from the target Stat according to the filtering rules defined by the Modifier Remover Effect.

docs.title: Set
docs.description: The Set Processor calculates the Effect value according to the processed Effect's configuration and directly sets the target Stat's RawValue to the resulting value.

docs.title: Conditional Modifier
docs.description: The Conditional Modifier Processor modifies the Effect value according to the conditions and contribution rules defined by the resolved Conditional Modifier Stat and its Signature.

docs.title: Critical Hit
docs.description: The Critical Hit Processor determines whether the incoming Effect becomes a critical hit and, if successful, applies a multiplier contribution to the Effect value.

docs.title: Modifier
docs.description: The Modifier Processor modifies the Effect value according to the contribution rules defined by the resolved Modifier Stat and its Signature.

docs.title: Multiplier Against Suppressor
docs.description: The Multiplier Against Suppressor Processor calculates a multiplier based on the relationship between the incoming Attribute Stat and its corresponding Suppressor Stat, then adds the result to the Effect's Contribution Data as a Multiplier contribution.

docs.title: Feedback
docs.description: Feedback Processors are responsible for generating gameplay feedback after an Effect has been processed.

docs.title: Standard
docs.description: Description

docs.title: Block By Result Attribute
docs.description: The Block By Result Attribute Processor blocks an Effect according to the Result Attributes added during the processing pipeline.

docs.title: Limiter
docs.description: The Limiter Processor is available for Numerable Stats.

docs.title: Min Max Offset
docs.description: The Min Max Offset Processor adds a percentage offset to the calculated Effect value based on the Minimum and Maximum Offset Stats.

docs.title: Reflector
docs.description: The Reflector Processor redirects the final calculated Effect value to one or more Actors according to the configuration defined by the resolved Reflector Stat and its Signature.

docs.title: Shield
docs.description: The Shield Processor consumes the value of a Shield Stat before the calculated Effect value is applied to the target Stat.

docs.title: Counter Effect
docs.description: The Standard Postprocess Counter Effect is the signed version of the Postprocess Counter Effect Processor.

docs.title: Modifier Replacer
docs.description: The Modifier Replacer is the signed version of the Postprocess Modifier Replacer Processor.

docs.title: Cast Restrictor
docs.description: The Cast Restrictor is the signed version of the Preprocess Cast Restrictor Processor.

docs.title: Counter Effect
docs.description: The Counter Effect is the signed version of the Preprocess Counter Effect Processor.

docs.title: Director
docs.description: The Director is the signed version of the Preprocess Director Processor.

docs.title: Effect Attribute Eliminater
docs.description: The Effect Attribute Eliminater is the signed version of the Preprocess Effect Attribute Eliminater Processor.

docs.title: Hit Rate
docs.description: The Hit Rate is the signed version of the Preprocess Hit Rate Processor.

docs.title: Immune
docs.description: Description

docs.title: Modifier Count Limit
docs.description: The Modifier Count Limit is the signed version of the Preprocess Modifier Count Limit Processor.

docs.title: Reflector
docs.description: It allows reflection to be performed according to the incoming Effect value sign and redirects the final calculated Effect value to one or more Actors based on the configuration defined by the resolved Reflector Stat and its Signature.

docs.title: Effect Timer Modifier
docs.description: The Effect Timer Modifier Processor processes Effect Timer Modifier Effects and modifies the remaining duration of active Modifiers.

docs.title: Container
docs.description: Each Container stores Stat Definitions of a specific type.

docs.title: Environment
docs.description: A Stat Environment groups different types of Stat Containers together.

docs.title: Repository
docs.description: A Stat Repository holds one or more Stat Environments.

docs.title: Actor
docs.description: An Actor Stat stores a reference to an Actor.

docs.title: Conditional Modifier
docs.description: A Conditional Modifier is a Modifier that is executed only when its condition is satisfied.

docs.title: Duration Modifier
docs.description: A Duration Modifier Stat modifies an Effect's duration during the Duration Process.

docs.title: Effect Bundle
docs.description: An Effect Bundle Stat inherits from Effect Pack Stat.

docs.title: Effect Pack
docs.description: An Effect Pack Stat stores one or more Effect Packs.

docs.title: Event Based Effect
docs.description: An Event Based Effect Stat triggers an Effect when a specific event occurs.

docs.title: Generator
docs.description: A Generator Stat automatically generates or consumes its value over time.

docs.title: Stat Definition
docs.description: A Stat Definition is a data asset that defines a Stat's values, properties, and behavior.

docs.title: Limiter
docs.description: A Limiter Stat limits an incoming Effect value based on configurable rules.

docs.title: Modifier
docs.description: A Modifier defines logic that modifies an Effect's value.

docs.title: Clamped
docs.description: A Clamped Stat restricts its final Value to remain within a minimum and maximum range.

docs.title: EdgeValue
docs.description: An EdgeValue Stat is a specialized Clamped Stat whose Value is determined by the selected border of its candidate values.

docs.title: Numerable
docs.description: A Numerable Stat is the base type for all numeric Stats.

docs.title: Counter Effect
docs.description: A Counter Effect is a Redirector that triggers another Effect in response to an incoming Effect.

docs.title: Director
docs.description: A Director is a Redirector that redirects the Effect itself during the Preprocess or Precontribution stages.

docs.title: Redirector
docs.description: A Redirector is the base class for components that redirect incoming Effects during processing.

docs.title: Reflector
docs.description: A Reflector is a Redirector that redirects an Effect's final value after it has been fully processed.

docs.title: State Based Effect
docs.description: A State Based Effect Stat automatically triggers an Effect when the configured Stat condition is satisfied.

docs.title: String
docs.description: A String Stat stores text values.

docs.title: Vector
docs.description: A Vector Stat stores a vector value.

docs.title: Stat Key Model
docs.description: A Stat Key Model uniquely identifies the location of a Stat within a Stat Repository.

docs.title: StatKeyModel_SigSep
docs.description: A StatKeyModel_SigSep uniquely identifies a Stat stored inside a SigSep Stat.

docs.title: Diplomacy
docs.description: DiplomacySettings contains the configuration used by the DiplomacyController. It defines settings such as the TeamStatKey and other diplomacy-related configuration values.

docs.title: World
docs.description: PowerWorldSettingsModel contains the configuration used to initialize PowerWorld. It defines how Power's core runtime systems, such as Diplomacy, Updater, and Logging, are configured.

docs.title: Log
docs.description: PowerLogSettings controls Power's runtime logging system. It allows you to enable or disable logs, filter log categories, and display logs only for specific runtime objects.

docs.title: Updater
docs.description: Updater controllers are responsible for updating time-dependent Power elements, such as Effects, Effectors, and Stats. They provide the update flow used by systems that rely on time or turn progression.

docs.title: Actor
docs.description: Actors are runtime entities that contain Stats and participate in Effect interactions.

docs.title: Effector
docs.description: An Effector is responsible for sending Effects to a target.

docs.title: PowerWorld
docs.description: PowerWorld is initialized using PowerWorldSettings and serves as the central hub for Power's runtime systems, such as Diplomacy, Updater, and Logging.

docs.title: Effect Process Flow
docs.description: When an Effect is sent in Power, it is processed by a series of Processors based on its type and configuration.

docs.title: How Stat Values Are Evaluated
docs.description: Several elements contribute to a stat's final value.

docs.title: Log Filtering
docs.description: To enable Power's debug logs, the POWER_DEBUG scripting define symbol must be added to your project's Scripting Define Symbols.

docs.title: Relative Stats for Target Stats and Attribute Stats
docs.description: A Stat can participate in an Effect in two different roles:

docs.title: Signature Seperated Stats
docs.description: A Signature Separated Stat is a container Stat that holds multiple child Stats.

docs.title: Source Seperated Stats
docs.description: A Source Separated Stat is both a regular Stat and a container Stat.

docs.title: Using Conditional Modifiers in Processors
docs.description: Some Processors expose UseConditionalModifier options. These allow a Stat used by the Processor to be modified dynamically according to the current context before the Processor continues its execution.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.description: Create a doc page with rich content.

frontMatter.description: Create a doc page with rich content.

docs.title: Editor
docs.description: The Power Editor is the main window you'll use while working with Power.

docs.title: What is Power
docs.description: Create a doc page with rich content.

frontMatter.description: Create a doc page with rich content.

docs.title: 3DCells
docs.description: 3DCells is a demo scene that showcases how Power can be used in a real-time action game.

docs.title: Enter Exit
docs.description: Applies an Effect when a target enters the collision area and removes the Effect when the target leaves it.

docs.title: Effector
docs.description: Before continuing, read the Effector documentation to understand how Effectors work.

docs.title: On Collide
docs.description: Applies Effects when a collision occurs.

docs.title: On Stay
docs.description: Continuously applies Effects while a target remains inside the collision area.

docs.title: General Expansions
docs.description: General Expansions contains systems and extensions that are not part of the Power Core but significantly extend Power's capabilities.

docs.title: Casting Model
docs.description: A CastingModel defines how a Skill is cast.

docs.title: Skill
docs.description: The Skill system is designed to support modern gameplay mechanics such as multi-state skills, different input systems, animation systems, and animation canceling. It is highly dynamic and extensible, allowing it to adapt to a wide variety of game designs.

docs.title: Skill State
docs.description: A SkillState defines how a Skill behaves while it is active. It controls the activation flow, casting behavior, targeting, and the Effects triggered throughout the Skill lifecycle.

docs.title: Targeting Model
docs.description: A TargetingModel defines how a Skill delivers its Effects to the target.

docs.title: Samples
docs.description: Samples include additional tools and features for Power, such as General Expansions, as well as demo projects like 3DCells and Slay The Sphere.

docs.title: Slay The Sphere
docs.description: Slay The Sphere is a demo scene that showcases how Power can be used to build a turn-based card game.

link.description: Learn the most important Power concepts.
Power have various editor windows to help you to create and manage your assets easily.
Stat Keys are used to access Stats on Actors. They provide a layered access mechanism similar to the container hierarchy used to store Stats.

default.title: Markdown page example
default.description: You don't need React to write simple standalone pages.
